mnemonics: CRESCENT: Computing Resources Evolve, Scale, and Need Evaluation Now & Tomorrow
keyPoints: Moore's Law historically drove exponential growth in processing power, but its pace has slowed, requiring new strategies.
The shift is from scaling up (vertical - bigger servers) to scaling out (horizontal - more servers/cloud instances).
Evolution now focuses on software-defined infrastructure, containerization, and leveraging specialized hardware like GPUs.
commonMistakes: Assuming past performance trends (like Moore's Law) will continue indefinitely without planning for architectural shifts.
Focusing only on immediate resource needs without forecasting future business growth or technological changes.
Neglecting the total cost of ownership (TCO) when evaluating new resource paradigms like cloud vs. on-premise.
